interpretation: Only a very small fraction of internal bonds broke by 60 micrometres.

interpretation: Damage is confined to one mother pebble in the corrected pilot.
boundary: Do not infer converged probability from one corrected pilot.

interpretation: The damaged mother pebble is top-near, but not simply the geometrically highest pebble.

interpretation: The native force graph densifies before reorganizing around the microcracking sequence.
boundary: Topology ratio is a finite-bed mechanism descriptor, not a material constant.

interpretation: The top-loaded force component recruits more mother pebbles before later reorganization.

interpretation: The endpoint graph has fewer active inter-mother force edges than the maximum sampled state.
boundary: Use as evidence of reorganization, not monotonic damage accumulation.

interpretation: The pilot exhibits peak-to-endpoint load relaxation after event-localized bond loss.

interpretation: The fractured pilot carries a larger final inter-pebble force sum than the intact independent bed.
boundary: This is a local force-path contrast, not a universal threshold.

evidence: pilot native-force endpoint and event table
interpretation: The bed remains force-connected while one mother pebble has localized internal bond loss.
boundary: Connected force graph does not imply unchanged thermal or purge-flow transport.

interpretation: Lowering the bond-strength multipliers in the independent bed did not trigger damage by 60 micrometres.
boundary: Do not infer that strength is irrelevant; the result shows local force-path sensitivity in this bed.
